title: AWeber vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare AWeber and Constant Contact. We break down pricing, features, and ease of use to help you choose the right email marketing tool.
h1: AWeber vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Both AWeber and Constant Contact are established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and business models. AWeber targets solopreneurs and bloggers with a generous free tier, while Constant Contact caters to nonprofits and local businesses with a paid-first approach. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing needs and budget.

comparison_table.tool_a: Autoresponders, RSS-to-email, landing pages, AMP emails
comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, social media ads, e-commerce

choose_a_if: You're bootstrapping and want a genuinely free option to start with
You need advanced autoresponders and RSS-to-email functionality
You value simplicity and ease of use over extensive features
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need built-in shopping features
You want integrated event management and SMS capabilities
You're a local business or nonprofit needing social media ad tools

faq.q: Is AWeber better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. AWeber wins for solopreneurs who want a free tier and simplicity, while Constant Contact excels for businesses needing advanced features like SMS, event management, and e-commerce tools. AWeber has stronger customer ratings overall, particularly on Trustpilot.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, AWeber or Constant Contact?
faq.a: AWeber's free tier makes it cheaper for small lists (under 500 subscribers), costing nothing to start. Constant Contact's lowest paid plan is $12/month, so it's more cost-effective than AWeber's $15/month tier once you outgrow the free option. For growing businesses, the pricing becomes comparable.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to AWeber?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list imports and exports. You can download your contacts from Constant Contact and upload them to AWeber with minimal friction. The migration process typically takes a few minutes, though it's wise to test your automation rules after switching.
